title: VPC security guardrails Terraform module: SGs, NACLs, private DNS, egress policies
preview: Create a reusable, audit-ready Terraform module (registry-style) that implements Redwood private/VPC security guardrails: managed security group templates, stateless/ stateful NACL patterns, private hosted zone / split-horizon DNS integration, and restrictive egress policies with configurable passthroughs. The modul...
text:
Create a reusable, audit-ready Terraform module (registry-style) that implements Redwood private/VPC security guardrails: managed security group templates, stateless/ stateful NACL patterns, private hosted zone / split-horizon DNS integration, and restrictive egress policies with configurable passthroughs. The module must be compatible with our Private deployment automation (VPC creation, subnets, and routing) and provide clear variables for environment-specific overrides, explicit deny-by-default behavior, and out-of-band exceptions for partner integrations. Deliverables: 1) Terraform module source with examples, 2) upgrade guide for existing stacks, 3) health-check plans and unit/integration tests, 4) documentation for Console and onboarding engineers, 5) automated policy checks (Sentinel/OPA) and CI linting.

Goals and constraints:
- Provide secure defaults (least privilege egress,...

title: Audit log schema: include authz decision context + ensure SIEM export compatibility
preview:
Context / problem
Enterprise customers (Dedicated + Private) require evidence-grade auditability of authorization enforcement. Today we log “what happened” (API call / admin action), but we do not consistently log the authorization decision that allowed/denied the action. This is a gap for SOC2 evidence, incident re...
text:
Context / problem
Enterprise customers (Dedicated + Private) require evidence-grade auditability of authorization enforcement. Today we log “what happened” (API call / admin action), but we do not consistently log the authorization decision that allowed/denied the action. This is a gap for SOC2 evidence, incident response, and SIEM correlation (“why was access denied/allowed, under which policy version?”).

This ticket covers:
1) Extending audit log schema to include authz decision context.
2) Ensuring the audit-log exporter includes the same context (and maps cleanly to common SIEM fields).
3) Rollout and backward compatibility (don’t break existing consumers).

Goals
- Every security-relevant request that hits an authz check should emit an audit event with authz decision context (allow/deny) + minimal explanation suitable for operators.
- Decision context must be consistent across H...

title: Observability-driven acceptance for streamed function-call flows and failure classification
preview: Define clear, testable acceptance criteria for structured output and function/tool-calling behavior in streaming responses, with an emphasis on observable failure classification and graceful degradation. This ticket captures requirements that the runtime, gateway, and SDKs must satisfy before we mark the feature GA....
text: Define clear, testable acceptance criteria for structured output and function/tool-calling behavior in streaming responses, with an emphasis on observable failure classification and graceful degradation. This ticket captures requirements that the runtime, gateway, and SDKs must satisfy before we mark the feature GA. The goal is to protect downstream consumers (apps and analytics) from silently corrupted or partial tool invocations and to provide deterministic signals for operators to triage issues quickly.

title: Proxy admission fallback and snowball protection for tenant SLO compliance
preview: Summary: Implement a proxy-level admission controller that provides graceful fallback and 'snowball protection' during limiter outages and cross-region failover. The controller sits in front of regional throttlers and enforces soft admission windows, spillback policies, and tenant smoothing to prevent cascading retr...
text:
Summary: Implement a proxy-level admission controller that provides graceful fallback and 'snowball protection' during limiter outages and cross-region failover. The controller sits in front of regional throttlers and enforces soft admission windows, spillback policies, and tenant smoothing to prevent cascading retry storms and preserve SLOs for high-priority customers.

Background: We've seen two classes of incidents: 1) local limiter service instability that causes a sharp drop in admission tokens for many tenants, and 2) global retry storms where failed or partially-admitted requests cascade into other regions (the "snowball" effect). Existing limiters are excellent at per-node enforcement but lack a lightweight proxy layer to coordinate temporary backpressure, spillback, and continuum-mode rollbacks while preserving fairness across tenants and regions.

Goals:
- Maintain 99.9% req...

title: Audit Console: journey replay + contextual download bundles
preview: Add an 'Audit Journey' feature in the Console that lets admins and authorized auditors: run precise search queries, step through a time-ordered sequence of related audit events (replay), and produce contextual download bundles (CSV/JSON/NDJSON) that include event rows plus linked metadata snapshots (user role at tim...
text: Add an 'Audit Journey' feature in the Console that lets admins and authorized auditors: run precise search queries, step through a time-ordered sequence of related audit events (replay), and produce contextual download bundles (CSV/JSON/NDJSON) that include event rows plus linked metadata snapshots (user role at time, affected resources, request headers redacted per policy). Work ties into RBAC v2 to ensure only allowed roles can replay or download sensitive bundles. Deliver both an interactive UI for on-screen investigation and an async export job API for large result sets.

title: Audit event schema v2: add stable event_id + category taxonomy (compat plan included)
preview:
## Context
SIEM-ready audit export depends on a canonical, stable audit event schema with:
- A stable, globally unique `event_id` suitable for downstream deduplication (at-least-once delivery).
- A category taxonomy to support filtering (UI + API) and SIEM field mapping.
- A compatibility plan for existing audit log...
text:
## Context
SIEM-ready audit export depends on a canonical, stable audit event schema with:
- A stable, globally unique `event_id` suitable for downstream deduplication (at-least-once delivery).
- A category taxonomy to support filtering (UI + API) and SIEM field mapping.
- A compatibility plan for existing audit log consumers (internal pipelines + early enterprise exports).

This ticket covers schema changes in the core audit event emission path (Redwood control plane + services emitting audit events). Exporter-side delivery semantics are addressed in ADR(s) and exporter tickets, but we must land the schema foundation first.

## Goals
1) Add `event_id` to every audit event (required in v2) and guarantee stability.
2) Introduce `category` (and optional `sub_category`) taxonomy with clear definitions.
3) Maintain compatibility for existing consumers during the migration window.
4) Ensur...
tags: Enterprise Security & Compliance Foundation — SIEM-ready audit export (Beta)

title: Console scope rebalance: prioritize per-route cost quickcards and lightweight trace sampler
preview: Context: Our original Console launch scope included a full per-route analytics dashboard, deep trace visualization, and a detailed cost-breakdown table. With limited eng bandwidth and telemetry storage cost concerns, we are rebalancing to land a compact, high-impact surface that gives product teams actionable signal...
text: Context: Our original Console launch scope included a full per-route analytics dashboard, deep trace visualization, and a detailed cost-breakdown table. With limited eng bandwidth and telemetry storage cost concerns, we are rebalancing to land a compact, high-impact surface that gives product teams actionable signal while deferring heavier investments. This ticket captures the scope cuts, acceptance criteria, and rollout constraints for the trimmed deliverable: two 'quickcards' (per-route RPS/median latency and per-route token cost estimate) plus a lightweight trace sampling UI that links to full traces in observability backends.

title: Reduce join bloat by introducing shadow lookup tables and migration plan
preview: Problem: Several control-plane read paths (tenant lookups, project->resource joins) perform wide multi-stage joins against large, frequently updated lookup tables. These joins cause high p95 read latency, lock churn during maintenance, and index bloat. Background analysis shows top offending queries hit the lookup g...
text:
Problem: Several control-plane read paths (tenant lookups, project->resource joins) perform wide multi-stage joins against large, frequently updated lookup tables. These joins cause high p95 read latency, lock churn during maintenance, and index bloat. Background analysis shows top offending queries hit the lookup graph via three separate foreign-key resolutions (tenant -> org -> membership) and repeatedly scan large JSON payload columns for metadata predicates.

Goal: Reduce tail latency and IOPS by introducing compact 'shadow' lookup tables that denormalize only the join keys and frequently queried predicates (tiny column set). Use a conservative migration strategy: writable shadow tables kept in-sync via asynchronous backfill + streaming replay, ability to fall back to original joins while rolling out. Deliver a measurable p95 improvement and reduce index size on the primary lookup...

title: Long-tail quantization sweep and regression watchdog for latency-sensitive workloads
preview: Goal: build and run a long-tail (heavy-tail) performance sweep for quantization profiles (FP16 / INT8 / INT4) focused on latency SLOs (p50/p95/p99) and throughput (tokens/sec) while simultaneously measuring generation quality regressions for representative production prompts. Deliver a regression watchdog that can d...
text:
Goal: build and run a long-tail (heavy-tail) performance sweep for quantization profiles (FP16 / INT8 / INT4) focused on latency SLOs (p50/p95/p99) and throughput (tokens/sec) while simultaneously measuring generation quality regressions for representative production prompts. Deliver a regression watchdog that can detect subtle quality degradation correlated with perf wins and produce actionable rollbacks or model-variant fallbacks.

Scope: harness + dataset selection + metrics + regression detector + alerting + dashboards + playbook. This ticket covers the engineering work to extend the existing benchmarking harness to:
 * run extended tail-load scenarios (bursty arrival distributions and multi-tenant mixes)
 * capture per-request metadata (batch size, KV cache hit/miss, kernel type)
 * compute tokens/sec (aggregate & per-workload) and latency quantiles (p50/p90/p95/p99) at fine grai...

title: TTL enforcement for audit logs + integration points for compliance exports
preview:
Context
Audit logs are a core compliance artifact for Dedicated + Private customers. We need productized retention enforcement (TTL + purge semantics) for the audit log store, wired to the retention policy evaluation path, and compatible with evidence exports (audit-log-exporter + compliance report pack generator).
...
text:
Context
Audit logs are a core compliance artifact for Dedicated + Private customers. We need productized retention enforcement (TTL + purge semantics) for the audit log store, wired to the retention policy evaluation path, and compatible with evidence exports (audit-log-exporter + compliance report pack generator).

This ticket covers:
1) Enforcing retention for audit log storage (scheduled purge + verification signals).
2) Ensuring exporter/report generation can reliably produce time-windowed exports without being surprised by TTL behavior.

Non-goals
- Building a full custom report builder.
- Arbitrary per-field redaction (handled under logging standards).

Scope / deliverables
A. Data model + evaluation wiring
- Audit log storage must have an enforceable retention signal per tenant/environment.
- Source of truth for TTL: retention policy evaluation in control plane (per ADR-017).
-...

title: Admin Snaglet: proactive prober that converts detected support signals into safe runbook tasks
preview: Problem: On-call engineers spend significant time triaging recurring low-variety incidents that follow a predictable remediation path. Current runbooks exist as docs and occasionally as manual task templates; we lack a lightweight, auditable bridge that can detect a signal, create a safe, permissioned runbook task, ...
text:
Problem: On-call engineers spend significant time triaging recurring low-variety incidents that follow a predictable remediation path. Current runbooks exist as docs and occasionally as manual task templates; we lack a lightweight, auditable bridge that can detect a signal, create a safe, permissioned runbook task, and optionally execute idempotent remediation steps in a controlled manner.

Proposal: Build "Admin Snaglet", a small probe-and-task system that: (1) runs configurable probes (metrics, log patterns, synthetic checks), (2) converts positive probes into a runbook-as-task (typed inputs, allowed actions), (3) attaches a safety harness (dry-run, permission gate, TTL, approval requirement), and (4) integrates with our admin microtask runner to allow one-click or automated remediation. The goal is to reduce time-to-first-action for common incidents while preserving auditability an...

title: Speculative-decoding prune: prevent double-charging when speculative tokens are pruned at finalization
preview: Background: We introduced speculative decoding (speculative sampling + early token emission) to reduce latency for cold requests. The runtime emits speculative token fragments that may later be pruned or replaced during finalization (e.g., when a higher-probability re-sampled beam replaces earlier speculative tokens...
text: Background: We introduced speculative decoding (speculative sampling + early token emission) to reduce latency for cold requests. The runtime emits speculative token fragments that may later be pruned or replaced during finalization (e.g., when a higher-probability re-sampled beam replaces earlier speculative tokens). Problem: current metering sometimes charges both the speculative fragments and the final tokens when pruning/merge occurs, producing double-billing on a subset of mid-stream handoffs and pruning events. This ticket formalizes invariants, implements deterministic ledger adjustments at finalization, and instruments end-to-end tests to ensure no customer is billed for speculative tokens that were not included in the final delivered completion.

title: CI pipeline templating for stepwise pin + crypto-signed profile promotion
preview:
Problem: Model onboarding today is a mix of manual steps and bespoke CI jobs per-model. Engineers sign artifacts by hand, publish performance profiles manually, and then perform an ad-hoc pin/canary rollout. This increases lead time, causes inconsistent provenance records, and makes rollbacks error-prone.

Goal: Del...
text:
Problem: Model onboarding today is a mix of manual steps and bespoke CI jobs per-model. Engineers sign artifacts by hand, publish performance profiles manually, and then perform an ad-hoc pin/canary rollout. This increases lead time, causes inconsistent provenance records, and makes rollbacks error-prone.

Goal: Deliver a reusable, parameterized CI pipeline template that standardizes model build -> attest/sign -> publish-profile -> stepwise promote(pin) flow. Pipeline should produce cryptographic provenance (cosign/rekor), create version pins in the release broker only after automated canary evaluation passes, and emit audit-friendly logs. The pipeline must be usable for hosted and private onboarding flows and compatible with our model-registry artifacts and performance profile manifest format.

Scope: Implement templated YAML and helper scripts in platform repo, wire into GitHub Acti...

title: Permission inventory + endpoint audit across gateway/control plane (RBAC v2 coverage report)
preview:
Context
We need a canonical inventory of security-relevant actions (endpoints + async jobs) across Hosted API, Control Plane, Console backend, Dedicated admin, and Private admin surfaces. This ticket covers the engineering audit of what exists today (RBAC v1 and ad-hoc checks), mapping each action to an RBAC v2 perm...
text:
Context
We need a canonical inventory of security-relevant actions (endpoints + async jobs) across Hosted API, Control Plane, Console backend, Dedicated admin, and Private admin surfaces. This ticket covers the engineering audit of what exists today (RBAC v1 and ad-hoc checks), mapping each action to an RBAC v2 permission string, and producing an initial coverage report (what is already enforced vs missing vs inconsistent).

Goals
- Enumerate sensitive endpoints/jobs across:
 - API Gateway routes (public + admin)
 - Control Plane services (org/user/role, keys/secrets, routing policy, model pinning/rollouts, export)
 - Console backend (admin tooling endpoints)
 - Dedicated/Private admin operations (cluster settings, upgrades, install/bootstrap, audit export)
- Map each action to an RBAC v2 permission string per ADR-014 naming/versioning.
- Identify and classify gaps:
 - Missing authz c...

title: Adaptive tenant-aware audit sampling and epoched index compaction
preview:
Goal: Reduce long-term audit storage costs while preserving forensic value and RBAC traceability by introducing tenant-aware probabilistic sampling at ingestion and an epoched index compaction strategy for coldstore.

Background: Audit ingestion has grown 3x this year; storing full indexes for all tenants has become...
text:
Goal: Reduce long-term audit storage costs while preserving forensic value and RBAC traceability by introducing tenant-aware probabilistic sampling at ingestion and an epoched index compaction strategy for coldstore.

Background: Audit ingestion has grown 3x this year; storing full indexes for all tenants has become the dominant driver of cost. Existing tiering is reactive (hot/cold cutoff by age) and causes large coldstore bills for a small subset of high-volume tenants. We need a deterministic, explainable approach that: (1) preserves high-fidelity logs for high-risk tenants and admin actions, (2) reduces indexing footprint for low-value, high-volume noise, and (3) enables efficient query performance for common forensic patterns.

Proposal overview: Two complementary mechanisms.
1) Adaptive Tenant-aware Sampling (ATS) at ingest: a decision layer that applies probabilistic downsampli...
